title: Jacket potato with chilli con carne
subtitle: A crisp-skinned baked potato piled with a rich beef and kidney-bean chilli
excerpt: Leftover chilli poured over a hot jacket potato is one of the best uses of batch-cooked mince. The contrast of the crisp skin, fluffy flesh, and spiced chilli with cold soured cream is a combination that earns its place as a weeknight staple.
sourceNotes: Standard British midweek combination of jacket potato and chilli. Chilli con carne in the British home tradition uses mince, tinned tomatoes, and kidney beans, differing from the Tex-Mex and Mexican originals. Combined with a jacket potato, this is a widely-shared weeknight pairing across British households.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The chilli can be made 2 days ahead and kept chilled. Make a full batch and use leftovers on jacket potatoes throughout the week.

content.text: Jacket potato with chilli is a filling weeknight dinner that uses two British staples together. The jacket potato is baked in the usual way while the chilli simmers on the hob. Use the potato-baking time to make the chilli from scratch, or use batch-cooked chilli kept in the fridge.

items.prepNote: large floury variety, about 300 g each

content.text: Bake the potatoes and make the chilli
Heat the oven to 220°C fan. Prick, oil, and salt the potatoes and place on the oven rack. Bake for 70 to 75 minutes until the skin is crisp and the flesh is tender.

While the potatoes bake, heat a little oil in a saucepan. Cook the onion for 8 minutes until softened. Add the garlic, chilli powder, and cumin and cook for 1 minute. Add the mince and cook, breaking it up, until browned.
Stir in the tomato puree, then add the tinned tomatoes and kidney beans. Season and simmer for 30 to 40 minutes until the chilli is thick and the sauce has deepened in colour.
Cut a cross in each potato and open it up. Spoon a generous ladleful of chilli over each potato. Add a dollop of soured cream and a sprinkle of grated cheddar if you like.

items.fix: Remove the lid and simmer on a high heat for 10 minutes until thickened. Watery chilli on a baked potato floods the plate.

items.cause: Chilli strength varies widely between brands and types.
items.fix: Add a teaspoon of sugar or a squeeze of tomato ketchup. A dollop of soured cream on top also cuts the heat when serving.

items.cause: Foil was used, or the temperature was too low.
items.fix: Bake uncovered, without foil, at a high temperature for the full time.

content.text: Chilli-topped jacket potato is a product of the British love of the jacket potato as a vehicle for anything hot and saucy. The combination became popular in British homes and canteens during the 1980s, when chilli con carne arrived as a weekly dinner staple. A cold dollop of soured cream on top is both practical and perfect.
